title: Cloud Breaches: AWS Incident Autopsies

modules.title: How Cloud Breaches Actually Happen: Anatomy of Real AWS Incidents
modules.sub: Dissecting Capital One, Twitch, and Toyota breach timelines
modules.description: Learners walk through the forensic timeline of three landmark AWS breaches, identifying the exact sequence of attacker actions from initial access to exfiltration. They map each incident to a common structural pattern, building a mental model for recognizing breach signatures in unfamiliar environments.

modules.title: Misconfiguration as the Root Cause: S3, IAM, and Beyond
modules.sub: Tracing how permissive configs become exploitable attack surfaces
modules.description: Learners audit representative S3 bucket policies and IAM role configurations drawn from real breach post-mortems, pinpointing the specific misconfigurations that created exploitable exposure. They practice categorizing findings by severity and blast radius so they can prioritize remediation decisions in their own environments.

modules.title: AI-Assisted Attacker Tooling in Cloud Environments
modules.sub: Understanding how AI accelerates cloud misconfiguration discovery at scale
modules.description: Learners examine how modern attackers use AI-assisted scanning tools to enumerate misconfigured AWS resources in minutes, reviewing real tooling behavior and output. They assess which of their own environment's exposed surfaces would be surfaced first by automated reconnaissance, sharpening their attacker-perspective threat modeling.

modules.title: Detection Engineering: Spotting Lateral Movement in AWS Logs
modules.sub: Reading CloudTrail and GuardDuty data to catch attackers mid-campaign
modules.description: Learners analyze annotated CloudTrail event sequences and GuardDuty findings from simulated breach scenarios, identifying the indicators that signal lateral movement, privilege escalation, and credential abuse. They write and refine detection logic rules that would surface these events as actionable alerts in a real environment.

modules.title: Incident Response Playbooks for Cloud-Native Environments
modules.sub: Building decision-ready response workflows specific to AWS breaches
modules.description: Learners evaluate existing generic IR playbooks against the unique constraints of cloud-native incidents — ephemeral resources, shared-responsibility boundaries, and API-driven forensics. They adapt and extend a cloud-specific playbook template, making explicit decisions about containment, evidence preservation, and stakeholder communication sequencing.

modules.title: Hardening Your AWS Posture: Guardrails That Would Have Helped
modules.sub: Applying preventive controls that each case study lacked
modules.description: Learners revisit the breach case studies from earlier modules and work backwards to identify the specific AWS-native controls — SCPs, bucket policies, CloudTrail configurations, and GuardDuty enablement — that would have prevented or significantly limited each incident. They produce a prioritized hardening checklist mapped directly to the failure patterns examined throughout the course.

modules.title: Tabletop Exercise: Respond to a Simulated S3 Exfiltration Event
modules.sub: Practicing real-time decisions under a live breach scenario
modules.description: Learners work through a structured tabletop scenario in which an S3 exfiltration event unfolds in stages, making time-pressured decisions about detection, containment, communication, and recovery at each phase. They debrief against a scoring rubric that assesses decision quality, playbook adherence, and identification of missed indicators, consolidating the full course into applied practice.

synopsis: High-profile AWS breaches — Capital One, Twitch, Toyota — share a pattern of preventable misconfigurations that attackers, now aided by AI scanning tools, find in minutes. This course dissects real incidents forensically so security engineers can recognize the same failure signatures in their own cloud environments before an attacker does.
rationale: AI-powered cloud scanning tools have dramatically accelerated attacker discovery of misconfigured cloud assets, yet most cloud security training remains abstract rather than grounded in the forensic detail of actual breaches.
learning_outcome: Learners can analyze AWS CloudTrail and GuardDuty data to identify breach indicators, map their own environment against the failure patterns found in real incidents, and execute a cloud-specific incident response playbook.
source_signals: Paige Thompson convicted over data theft from AWS user accounts
